title: [TDD] reconcile orphaned runs on boot -> 'interrupted'
description: On startup, any run in an active status (researching/building/checking/refining) with no live engine is orphaned. Mark it 'interrupted' (new RunStatus in shared contract) so the UI shows it resumable instead of 'researching' forever.

title: Durable & resumable runs (survive backend restart)
description: A run cut off mid-flight (researching/building/checking) is orphaned by a backend restart: fire-and-forget in-memory engine state is lost, status freezes at 'researching', no engine drives it. Make runs durable so a user can resume from the furthest checkpoint reached. Keystone: research text is not persisted today (the expensive web_search work is lost). Builds on 3142ab9 rehydrate(). Spec: resume from gates/checkpoints already reached.

title: [BUG] Worker selection is cosmetic: server builds ONE startup agent (defaults to opus-4-8); per-run workerId never selects the model (R11 swap broken)
description: server.ts calls createAgent({USE_REAL_AGENT,ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}) once at startup with NO workerId -> resolveWorker defaults to 'opus' (claude-opus-4-8). The shared agent is injected into composeRunDeps and used for ALL runs. run.service/run-engine thread the run's workerId only into telemetry/alarm labels (ctx.workerId), not into agent/model selection. Result: picking Sonnet (or any worker) still runs opus-4-8. Fix: create/select the agent per run from the run's workerId (honor R11 swap) so the form choice picks the model. Pairs with publisher-aisdk (opus-4-8 needs the SDK upgrade; sonnet-4-6 works today).
close_reason: Superseded by publisher-rrt.2 (Real Run Tooling — per-run worker selection)

title: [BUG] Opus-4.8 workers fail: @ai-sdk/anthropic@1.2.12 injects temperature (rejected). Upgrade AI SDK.

Raw API + key work fine; sonnet-4-6 & opus-4-6 work via the SDK. Fix: upgrade @ai-sdk/anthropic (1.2.12 -> 3.x) and ai (4 -> 6) — major bump with breaking changes (maxSteps -> stopWhen/stepCountIs in anthropic-agent.ts + anthropic-research-agent.ts), and it unlocks the real server-side web_search/web_fetch tools the research agent currently stubs. Stopgap: use the Sonnet-4.6 worker.

title: Bug: concurrent mint loses the idempotency race → 500 instead of returning the existing share
description:
WHERE: backend/src/services/share.service.ts mint() (lines 98-107).

SPEC: specs/008-publish-share-url/spec.md Edge Cases — "Concurrent mint on the same run → exactly one active share (unique constraint), second call returns the first."

WHAT/EXPECTED vs ACTUAL: mint() does getActiveByRun(runId) then shareStore.create(). In a true concurrent race, two callers both see null from getActiveByRun and both call create(). The DB partial unique index (idx_shares_active_run) correctly rejects the second insert — so data integrity (exactly one active share) IS guaranteed. BUT the service does NOT catch the thrown unique-constraint error. It propagates uncaught to the route (routes/share.ts:74), which maps it to a generic 500. Per the spec, the concurrent loser should instead re-read the now-existing active share and return THAT link (the same {slug,url}). Actual: concurrent loser gets HTTP 500.

TEST GAP: The unique-index throw is asserted at the STORE level (tests/unit/share-store.test.ts:116 "should REJECT a second active share"). The idempotency tests (share-mint.test.ts:109, share-service.test.ts:96) only exercise the SEQUENTIAL path, where the first mint has already committed before the second's getActiveByRun runs — so they never hit the create() throw. No test asserts the service catches the constraint violation and returns the existing share. The spec's concurrent edge case is therefore neither implemented nor tested.

REPRO (conceptual): with two callers interleaved as [A:getActiveByRun→null][B:getActiveByRun→null][A:create→ok][B:create→THROW], B currently throws -> 500. Reproduce in a unit test with a fake store whose getActiveByRun returns null on the first two calls but whose create throws on the second active insert; assert mint() returns the first share rather than throwing.

FIX (suggested): in mint(), wrap create() so that on a unique-constraint failure it re-reads getActiveByRun(runId) and returns linkFor(existing.slug); only rethrow if no active share is found. Add a service unit test for the race and an integration test if feasible.">

title: Review: createSlug _collisionAvoid redraw loop is dead code (Rule 8 simplicity)
description: WHERE: backend/src/util/slug.ts:21-29 (createSlug) and the only caller backend/src/composition.ts:131 (slug: createSlug). FINDING (Rule 8 Simplicity / Rule 4 dead complexity): createSlug takes an optional _collisionAvoid arg and runs an unbounded for(;;) redraw loop to avoid returning a token equal to it. But the sole production caller wires 'slug: createSlug' BARE — createShareService calls slug() with no arguments (share.service.ts:103), so _collisionAvoid is always undefined and 'slug !== undefined' is always true: the loop ALWAYS returns on the first iteration. The redraw branch is unreachable in all real and test paths (slug.ts shows 100% line coverage precisely because the loop body never iterates twice). This is speculative defense-in-depth for a collision that (a) cannot occur against undefined and (b) at ~144 bits of entropy needs no avoidance anyway. SUGGESTED FIX: drop the _collisionAvoid parameter and the loop; return randomBytes(SLUG_BYTES).toString('base64url') directly. If a future caller truly needs collision-avoidance against an existing-slug set, reintroduce it with a real argument and a bounded retry + test. Note: this does NOT weaken unguessability — entropy is unchanged.

title: Review: ShareSchema.slug validation does not enforce the documented ≥16-char url-safe contract
description: WHERE: shared/src/contracts/share.ts:13 — ShareSchema.slug is z.string().min(1). FINDING (Rule 2 Type Safety): the schema's own doc comment (line 13) and the spec (Locked decisions: 'unguessable, non-enumerable url-safe slug ≥16 chars') promise a ≥16-char, [A-Za-z0-9_-] slug, but the Zod schema only requires min(1). The real ≥16/alphabet gate lives ONLY in routes/share.ts SLUG_RE at lookup time. So a row written with a short or non-url-safe slug would pass ShareSchema.parse() in share.store.rowToShare() — the boundary validator (Rule 2) does not actually validate the documented invariant, it just rubber-stamps. Today createSlug always yields a 24-char url-safe token so no bad row exists, but the contract and the validator have drifted. SUGGESTED FIX: tighten ShareLink/Share slug to z.string().regex(/^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{16,}$/) (single source of truth shared with the route's SLUG_RE), or extract the slug regex to @publisher/shared and reuse it in BOTH ShareSchema and routes/share.ts SLUG_RE so mint-shape and lookup-shape are provably identical. Add a contract unit test asserting a <16-char or '.'-containing slug is REJECTED by ShareSchema.parse.

title: Review: createAgentForWorker thin alias adds no value — delete or document
Review: AnthropicResearchAgent.research() missing empty-sources coverage test after empty response
